mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in nonfarm payroll employment reflect expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS month-over-month changes fail to exhibit a positive rank correlation with subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns over any 2-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below 4.0 percent signals a late-cycle overheating labor market where wage pressures compress corporate margins and prompt Federal Reserve tightening, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 4.0%, the S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances, or the mean forward return is not statistically negative.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously decelerating industrial production and rising consumer prices generate a stagflationary impulse that compresses earnings multiples and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint behavior of INDPRO and CPIAUCSL fails to predict negative 20-day returns with a rank information coefficient below -0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During Federal Reserve tightening cycles, each increase in the federal funds rate raises discount rates applied to future corporate cash flows, reducing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods identified as monetary tightening cycles, positive 20-day S&P 500 returns occur in more than 50 percent of months despite rising FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales signals robust household consumption that translates directly into higher corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS month-over-month growth fails to correlate positively with subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns with a rank IC greater than 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production indicates improving manufacturing sector health and rising capacity utilization, which supports earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive momentum in INDPRO does not coincide with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55 percent of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 1.2 million annualized units signal a contraction in residential investment that drags down broad aggregate demand and construction-related earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST below 1200 coincides with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of triggered months over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP captures the broadest measure of aggregate demand expansion; positive surprises in GDP growth drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised upward.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth quartile fails to rank positively against subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns with a rank IC greater than 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.
